Title: Add Newsletter Builder
Description:
Take that week's newsletter, put a checkbox next to each section, and let users decide which pieces they want to include in their zone newsletter. ???	

PROMPT:


Newsletter builder
Spec out a web app for me that enables my volunteer neighborhood captains to create their own zone newsletters, drawing content from our Altogether town-wide newsletter, which comes out every two weeks. The format of our newsletter is built in something called Mailer Light, and it then just goes out as a big email to our list.

What I want is for neighborhood captains to be able to look at the newsletter, either check a box or click and drag the items that they want to add to their newsletter, and then add custom stuff if they want to:
- add a custom name for their newsletter
- add a little logo if they want to
- make the output something that's focused for their zone and small, and over which the neighborhood captain feels ownership


I want it to be low friction and easy for the neighborhood captain to do. I want them to feel empowered and excited to do this each week. It needs to be lightweight, slick, and have a really good-looking output that's then very easy for them to copy and paste into an email or generate or print out so that they can deliver it in person to places.

Spec out how this might work. It can be a standalone website/web app kind of thing, and then spec which I'll then link to from the zone dashboard. Also spec out: well, I don't know; tell me what you would need from me to make this happen. We need to think about what sorts of process changes might be needed from the newsletter editors to make this possible. We also need to consider that our newsletter editors are volunteers, and any solution here needs to be as easy as possible for them to do. Ok, in thinking about the editorial process for the newsletter, if it's possible, I don't know how we do this exactly, but maybe we can develop a web scraper feature that makes it easier for them to find news every week that people need.

Title: Add “person details” modal
Description:
Add “person details” modal — 
user can click any person’s name in the Details panel and a modal of that person pops up. Same dimensions and design as the newsletter modal and add record modal. This modal contains all the same information about the person as is shown in the Details panel, but it also includes additional features & options. While Details displays Quick Tags, the Person modal will display All Tags — Quick Tags plus additional checkboxes and dropdowns. (Note that what I am describing here includes a change to the Details panel, changing Tags to Quick Tags.)
Within this new Person modal (but not elsewhere!) there is an option to tag the person as a “former resident”, plus a notes field alongside that checkbox. When that box is checked, this person will then no longer appear listed in the Details panel. Instead, for addresses that have a former resident associated with it, a small header “Former Residents” will appear at the bottom of the panel, with a list of each former resident underneath. All of this text should be smaller and grayed out in comparison to the main text in the Details panel. Click on a name in the former residents list to open the Person modal where User could then un-mark the person as being a former resident, thereby returning them to the normal flow within the Details panel.
